detaileddescription.para: Generated from class MR::TransformVdbVolumeResult. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::TransformVdbVolumeResult named volume. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

Modifies a member variable of class MR::TransformVdbVolumeResult named volume. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter value might be preserved in this object in element volume. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it held previously in volume.

Returns a mutable pointer to a member variable of class MR::TransformVdbVolumeResult named volume. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::TransformVdbVolumeResult named boxFixed. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

Modifies a member variable of class MR::TransformVdbVolumeResult named boxFixed. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it held previously in boxFixed.

Returns a mutable pointer to a member variable of class MR::TransformVdbVolumeResult named boxFixed. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_TransformVdbVolumeResult_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_TransformVdbVolumeResult_DestroyArray(). Use MR_TransformVdbVolumeResult_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_TransformVdbVolumeResult_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Constructs MR::TransformVdbVolumeResult elementwise. The reference to the parameter volume might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_TransformVdbVolumeResult_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it held previously.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Generated from constructor MR::TransformVdbVolumeResult::TransformVdbVolumeResult. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_TransformVdbVolumeResult_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a heap-allocated instance of MR_TransformVdbVolumeResult. Does nothing if the pointer is null.
Destroys a heap-allocated array of MR_TransformVdbVolumeResult. Does nothing if the pointer is null.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::TransformVdbVolumeResult::operator=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in this object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it held previously.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_VdbVolume *volume, const MR_AffineXf3f *xf, const bool *fixBox, const MR_Box3f *box)
memberdef.name: MR_transformVdbVolume

detaileddescription.para:
Transform volume
volume
Volume to transform
xf
The transformation
fixBox
If true, and if box is valid and represents the bounding box of the volume, then the result will be shifted so that no data has negative coordinate by any of dimensions Generated from function MR::transformVdbVolume. Parameter volume can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter xf can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter fixBox has a default argument: false, pass a null pointer to use it. Parameter box is a single object. Parameter box has a default argument: {}, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_TransformVdbVolumeResult_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Same as above but for the SceneObject
true, if fixBox is true and the box was "fixed" (see parameter fixBox of transformVdbVolume) Generated from function MR::voxelsApplyTransform. Parameter obj can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter xf can not be null. It is a single object.
